comment_regular_data.body: The bug fix itself seems fine , but please read https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#ai-assisted-work

> The bug fix itself seems fine , but please read https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#ai-assisted-work

I've edited the PR as per the guidelines in the link.
my bad for merging :( @orbisai0security can you fine out why it fails with the zig build?

I see CI tests are already fixed.

Root cause of the CI failure: The test passed '4' * 512 to xxd -r, which runs through huntype(). That function accumulates address digits by left-shifting a signed long: want_off = (want_off << 4) | n1. After 17 hex digits with no colon separator, want_off overflows a signed 64-bit integer, undefined behaviour. Zig's ReleaseSafe build enables UBSan, which aborts with SIGILL (exit 132) on Linux and SIGABRT (exit 134) on macOS. That's the 132/134 the CI reported.
Is there a reason this did not go via Vim? Can any of you please backport those fixes to Vim please?
Hmm, `snprintf()` isn't available on all platforms, and `vim_snprintf()` isn't available in xxd.c either...
we could use `strncpy()` or even `memcpy` instead, no?

I cannot reproduce the overflow. How is this supposed to happen? xxd.c errors out when columns are too large, so how can it overflow? It seems like a false positive. In addition, the suggested test does not even go through `xxdline()`. #40246 seems valid, but seems to miss a case.

Related Vim PR: https://github.com/vim/vim/pull/20534
we have somewhat "forked" xxd.c so I don't expect Vim's version to have the same issues.

title: fix: add buffer-length check in xxd.c
